post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 160: hmmm

Story
hmmm


Read all of the rules.

Voting and Votekills
  • You must vote using the format of Vote: NAME. The name must be properly spelled (with a notable exception) and everything must be formatted correctly for the vote to count.
  • You may vote for no votekill to occur (thereby skipping the day) by doing Vote: Skip Day.
  • Any invalid vote will be completely ignored.
  • For the purposes of voting, the letters I (captial i) and l (lowercase L) will be considered the same; this goes for any other similar looking symbols.
  • If a player receives the majority of the votes, that player will be votekilled and the day will end immediately.
  • If at least half of the players vote to skip the day, then no votekill will occur and the day will end immediately.
  • If no one person receives the majority of the votes by the end of the day, the person with the most votes at that time will be votekilled. If there are multiple such people, then the person who got to that number of votes first gets votekilled.
  • There is no majority during Day 1. That means a votekill will only happen at the end of the day.
  • As soon as the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted.
  • Any incorrect votecounts made will be counted as the correct votecount if the mistake is not noticed and corrected within 24 hours.


Activity and Deadlines
  • Each day will be 2 weeks long.
  • Each night will be 3 days long.
  • I can extend the deadline if I deem it necessary, but do not expect me to do so every time.
  • I will give out PM Prods at my discretion; there is no official time limit, but I may start looking for a replacement as soon as 24 hours after I give a PM Prod.
  • If you post before I find a replacement (for your inactivity), you will not be replaced.


List of Don’t’s
  • Don’t post if you are not participating in this game.
  • Don’t post at night.
  • Don’t talk about this game to anyone except me (the mod) outside of this thread, unless you are given explicit permission by me (the mod) to do so.
  • Don’t edit or delete posts.
  • Don’t conceal text with things such as transparent text and the like. Hide tags are alright to use.
  • Don’t post if you are dead.
  • Don’t use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), purple (#8000BF), or anything that resembles one of those colors.
  • Don’t be immature.
  • Don’t gamethrow.
  • Don’t argue with the mod about warnings and modkills.
  • Don’t abuse loopholes.

Violation of any of these rules will result in a warning, a force-replacement, and/or a modkill. I reserve the right to give warnings to, force-replace, and/or modkill anyone for whatever reason.

Warnings
None!


Setup
  • There are 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Leader, 1 Town Co-Leader, and 2 Mafia Goons.
  • The Town Leader knows the identity of both mafia members.
  • The Town Co-Leader knows the identity of the Town Leader.
  • The Town Leader flips as a Vanilla Townie.
  • Mafia members may only discuss with each other privately at night in the designated quicktopic.
  • If both mafia members die, they have a chance to win by correctly guessing who the Town Leader is at the end of the game.

Role PMs
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a hmmm?, also known as a Vanilla Townie.

You have no night actions; your power comes from your freedom of speech and your vote.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening, provided that the mafia don’t correctly guess who the Town Leader is.
Town Co-Leader wrote:
You are hmmm..., also known as the Town Co-Leader.

You know that NAME is the Town Leader.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening, provided that the mafia don’t correctly guess who the Town Leader is.
Town Leader wrote:
You are hmmm!, also known as the Town Leader.

You know that NAME and NAME are both Mafia Goons. You are also known by the Town Co-Leader. You flip as a Vanilla Townie.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening, provided that the mafia don’t correctly guess who you are.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are hmm/hmmmm, also known as a Mafia Goon. Your partner is NAME, a Mafia Goon.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day, the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, nothing can prevent that from happening, or by correctly guessing who the Town Leader is at the end of the game.

Jeff attacked me for ISO'ing fishy and then voting math_turtle. If you actually read my ISO, you'd know that the conclusion I came to in it is that fishy isn't all that scummy. People sheeping jeff is triggering me, but they're just playing badly. Jeff, on the other hand, clearly assumes in making this attack that you only ISO people to prove them scum. This is the case if you are scum. In other words, jeff's attack is a tautology. If I'm scum, then I'm scum. But more importantly, jeff knows that you can use ISO's for purposes other than scumreading people, but he clearly ignores that when he makes his attack. What motivation does town jeff have to assume that my ISO is arriving at the conclusion that fishy is scum without even reading it? This is very scummy.

Vote: jeffisepic until he explains himself.

post_rendered:
Quote:
A "Straw man" argument (also called "setting up a straw man") involves mischaracterizing your opponent's position in order to present a weaker argument than they have actually given, thereby allowing you to defeat it. It usually involves subtle changes to the given facts of the matter, or minor changes to wording that lead to semantic differences in what is said.

Also, "Strawmanning" involves taking a small piece of the case someone has built up that is weaker than their other points and blowing it up. They can then defeat the weaker point and use that as 'proof' that everything that person has said is wrong.

If done skillfully, even the opponent will not realize what has gone wrong with their argument at first. In Mafia, this can give a Scummy player enough momentum to gain a lynch before anyone notices flaws in the new argument. If this is done in the Endgame, it can be a game-winning ploy. Otherwise, it is risky because many players may examine the argument anew during the Night phase, and find the holes in logic.

So here is an explanation of my actions on day 3.

So I had thought town leader flipeed, so I thought both leader and co leader were still alive. So I thought apw was either leader or co leader. Next, the town leader needs to make the mafia think that the leader is someone else, so when apw started pushing sudzoof and defending charge, I suspected that maybe apw was pulling a trick by intentionally townreading the scum. So, I came up with an idea to test whether this was the case. I started pushing Charge with very poor reasoning. If Charge was actually scum, I would also be bait for town leader pick. Now, if apw was indeed faking his charge townread, then I would have provided enough cover for him to vote charge while I bait the mafia pick. However, if apw did not jump of the wagon, then Charge would actually be town. So I waited until the last minute to jump off the wagon and defenestrate sudzoof.

Now, if apw was indeed faking his charge townread, then I would have provided enough cover for him to vote charge while I bait the mafia pick. However, if apw did not jump of the wagon, then Charge would actually be town. So I waited until the last minute to jump off the wagon and defenestrate sudzoof.
